note: SoA buffer layouts — the source rows for the generated BufferLayout keystone. Each buffer is a struct-of-arrays column store for ONE domain type, resident in the Rust worker's linear memory and mirrored read-only by JS. Columns are pure column-major: a column's data is `capacity` contiguous elements at `byteOffset`, so a typed-array view (Int32Array/Uint32Array/...) cuts over exactly one column with zero copy. Edit here; tooling/codegen emits the identical byte-offset table to Rust and TS so the writer and reader provably cannot drift.

buffers.note: One row per framing member emitted by the FramingSolver. Linear geometry is integer ticks (1/32in); coordinates are WORLD space (x/y in plan, z up) — the wall→world transform is composed at the bim-core composition root (ADR 0006/0012). Each member is a segment (start→end) plus a draw width and an enumerated role.

columns.note: Start point X (along the wall baseline), ticks.
Start point Y (through-wall depth), ticks; 0 in the elevation slice.
Section draw width (stroke thickness in elevation), ticks.
Index into `roles` below — the member's framing role, for coloring and picking.

buffers.note: The space-first plan-view source (ADR 0008): one row per footprint vertex. Vertices are a closed world-XY polygon ring (top-down plan plane is world XY), grouped by `spaceId` — the ring/space a vertex belongs to. Linear geometry is integer ticks (1/32in). This buffer is the plan view's entire data source; the 3D view extrudes it with `Volume.height`.

columns.note: Vertex X in the world-XY plan plane, ticks.
Vertex Y in the world-XY plan plane, ticks.
The space/footprint ring this vertex belongs to — groups consecutive vertices into one closed polygon.

buffers.note: The 3D massing-view source (ADR 0008): one row per extruded mass. With the matching `Footprint` ring it defines an extruded solid (base + top cap; side faces deferred). Linear geometry is integer ticks (1/32in).

columns.note: Stable id of this mass — the push/pull target the engine validates.
Extrude height above the base plane, ticks.
